Scenario.title: Cutting Edge Attack on Water and Wastewater System
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A basic-skilled threat group named "Cutting Edge" leverages phishing and script-based exploitation to compromise a water treatment facility's SCADA system, ultimately disrupting critical operational processes.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Script File, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-33063 (Suggested): Multiple Qualcomm chipsets contain a use-after-free vulnerability due to memory corruption in DSP Services during a remote call from HLOS to DSP.
stage.description: The attacker sends spear-phishing emails containing malicious attachments or links to employees, exploiting their lack of security awareness to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-39226 (Suggested): Grafana contains an authentication bypass vulnerability that allows authenticated and unauthenticated users to view and delete all snapshot data, potentially resulting in complete snapshot data loss.
stage.description: The attacker deploys a malicious PowerShell script via email attachment, which executes to establish persistence and prepare the environment for further exploitation.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker alters SCADA system parameters through manipulated data inputs, causing disruptions in water treatment processes such as chemical dosing or pump operations.

Scenario.summary: A basic-skilled group compromises a water treatment facility through phishing, executes malicious scripts, and manipulates critical system data to disrupt operations.
